personality_one_liner: A warm, approachable, and tactile interface that feels like finding your best friends, using soft radii, chunky cards, and vibrant teal/coral accents to reduce the anxiety of moving.

spacing.rule: Use generous padding. Cards should not be flush to the edge (min md margin). Group related elements clearly, leaving breathing room to make the UI feel relaxed.

do's: Use on the bottom tab bar when content scrolls behind it.
Use for sticky headers or bottom-sheet drag handles.
Use on floating action buttons (FABs) like the Pass/Like buttons overlaid on the swipe cards.
dont's: Do not use on form fields, chips, or body text areas.
Do not use without a tinted base layer underneath to guarantee contrast.

screens.name: Onboarding/Auth
screens.purpose: Quick, low-friction entry via mock OTP.

states.error: Inline text below input or snackbar

screens.layout: Centered large phone number input with a subtle borderStrong focus state. Sticky primary CTA ('Continue') pinned to the bottom above the keyboard. Top half of the screen can use a subtle brandTertiary background or abstract illustration.

screens.name: Profile Setup & Lifestyle Questionnaire
screens.purpose: Hinge-style onboarding flow to collect demographics and lifestyle habits one step at a time.
screens.layout: One question per screen. Large, friendly pill buttons/chips for options (e.g., Veg/Non-veg) grouped centrally. Progress bar at the top just below the safe area. Sticky primary CTA at the bottom. The UI should feel lightweight and expressive.

screens.name: Listing Creation
screens.purpose: Form to capture apartment details for users who have a place.
screens.layout: Vertical scrollable single-column form. Use large hit targets. A ₹ budget slider with clear value labels. Toggle chips for amenities. Sticky 'Post Listing' CTA at the bottom inside a KeyboardAvoidingView.

screens.name: Discover (Swipe)
screens.purpose: Tinder-style swipe cards to find compatible roommates.

states.empty: Illustration of an empty room with 'You have seen everyone!' and a button to widen filters
states.error: Error message 'Could not load profiles' with 'Retry' CTA

screens.layout: Hero screen. Full-screen cards stacked with slight overlap. The card is primarily an image with a strict gradient scrim (transparent to >=70% surfaceInverse) at the bottom 40%. Text over the scrim uses onSurfaceInverse and includes Name, Age, ₹ Budget, Locality, and a Compatibility Score badge. Floating circular action buttons (Pass, Like) sit at the bottom of the screen.

screens.name: Matches & Chat
screens.purpose: View mutual matches and engage in basic polling chat.

states.empty: Illustration showing two speech bubbles with 'No matches yet. Keep swiping!'

screens.layout: Top section: Horizontal scroll of 'New Matches' using avatars (real images or gradient initials). Bottom section: Vertical list of active chats. Chat screen has a sticky top header showing the user's name and compatibility summary, and a standard message list below.

screens.name: Profile/Settings
screens.purpose: Manage account, lifestyle tags, and preferences.
screens.layout: Top cover banner (tinted brandTertiary or user uploaded). Large avatar overlapping the banner bottom edge. List items below are grouped settings rows. Each row has a leading icon inside a tinted brandTertiary rounded square.

onboarding_bg.alt: Friends hanging out in an apartment

swipe_card_sample.alt: Modern Indian apartment interior living room

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement the Tinder-style swipe interface using react-native-reanimated and react-native-gesture-handler. Ensure cards stack smoothly with physical inertia.

MANDATORY: On the Swipe cards, where text overlays the background image, implement a gradient scrim at the bottom 40% going from transparent to 80% opacity surfaceInverse. Render all overlay text in onSurfaceInverse. Never place text directly on images without this scrim.

For seed profiles missing an image, use the fallback Initials Avatar: A pill/circle with a brandTertiary background and the user's initials centrally aligned in onBrandTertiary text. NO random system icons for users.

Build the Lifestyle Questionnaire mirroring Hinge's pattern: strictly one question per screen, use large chunky pill buttons/chips for answers, a top progress bar, and a sticky CTA anchored above the home indicator.
Provide specific haptic feedback: 'success' or 'light' for a right swipe (Like), 'rigid' for a left swipe (Pass), and 'selection' for tapping questionnaire chips.
Ensure safe area is respected on all screens. Content should never sit under the notch or home indicator. Sticky CTAs must factor in bottom safe area padding.
